perturbation.original_text: 3.0 The Parties shall limit disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its directors, officers, partners, members and/or employees having a need to know Confidential Information and shall not disclose Confidential Information to any third party (whether an individual, corporation, or other entity) without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.0 The Parties may disclose Confidential Information to any third party (whether an individual, corporation, or other entity) with or without the prior written consent of the other party, and such consent may be withheld for any reason. The Parties shall limit disclosure of Confidential Information within its own organization to its directors, officers, partners, members and/or employees having a need to know Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This modification reverses the restriction on disclosing Confidential Information to third parties. The original text forbids it without consent. The modified text allows disclosure with or without consent, creating a direct contradiction. The phrase 'such consent may be withheld for any reason' contradicts the implied requirement of 'not be unreasonably withheld' in the original text. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding disclosure obligations, and conflicts with Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.0 The confidential information to be disclosed under this Agreement (”Confidential Information”) can be described as and includes: technical and business information relating to proprietary ideas, patentable ideas and/or trade secrets, existing and/or contemplated products and services, research and development, production, costs, profit and margin information, finances and financial projections, customers, clients, marketing, and current or future business plans and models, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” or proprietary at the time of its disclosure. a. In addition to the above, Confidential Information shall also include, and the Parties shall have a duty to protect, other confidential and/or sensitive information which is (a) disclosed as such in writing and marked as confidential (or with other similar designation) at the time of disclosure; and/or (b) disclosed by in any other manner and identified as confidential at the time of disclosure and is also summarized and designated as confidential in a written memorandum delivered within thirty (30) days of the disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and creates a contradiction with Section 1. The original text restricts disclosure of Confidential Information to third parties without prior written consent. The changed text permits disclosure to third parties with or without consent. The changed text makes the 'duty to protect' Confidential Information outlined in Section 1 unenforceable because there's no longer a restriction on who the parties can disclose the Confidential Information to. The added phrase that 'such consent may be withheld for any reason' negates the original implied requirement of 'not being unreasonably withheld'. The change creates uncertainty and conflict regarding the obligations on disclosure of information, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.0 In the event of a breach, or threatened breach of any provision of this Agreement, the non-breaching party shall give the breaching party a notice to cure within three (3) business days. In the event the breach is not cured, the non-breaching party shall have the right to bring appropriate SPA DE SOLEIL, INC.​ ® MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT PAGE 2 v.20171020 legal action in the appropriate State or Federal court as set forth in Paragraph 9 above. The non-breaching party shall have the right to bring a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction and permanent injunction in addition to receiving any damages which the non-breaching party may prove in the appropriate litigation identified in Paragraph 9 above.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.0 In the event of a breach, or threatened breach of any provision of this Agreement, the non-breaching party may, at their sole discretion, give the breaching party a notice to cure within three (3) business days. The non-breaching party shall have no right to bring appropriate legal action in the appropriate State or Federal court as set forth in Paragraph 9 above. The non-breaching party shall have no right to bring a temporary restraining order, preliminary injunction and permanent injunction in addition to receiving any damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates a notice to cure and grants the right to legal action for a breach. The modified text makes the notice to cure discretionary ('may, at their sole discretion') and denies the right to legal action, directly contradicting the original terms. The section contradicts itself with Paragraph 9, which specifies the courts for legal disputes. This change introduces uncertainty regarding available remedies, and also affects section 11 if it's active.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9.0 The validity, construction and performance of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws, without regard to the laws as choice or conflict of laws, of the state of California. In the event of any dispute involving this Agreement, the parties hereby agree that such dispute shall be resolved in the appropriate State or Federal court, in the county of Los Angeles, state of California, and the parties hereby agree that proper venue and jurisdiction shall lie with any court of competent jurisdiction in the county of Los Angeles, state of California.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly denies the non-breaching party the right to bring legal action, including temporary restraining orders, preliminary injunctions, and permanent injunctions, in Paragraph 10. This directly contradicts Paragraph 9, which specifies that disputes shall be resolved in the appropriate State or Federal court in Los Angeles. By removing the right to legal action, the changed text makes Paragraph 9 unenforceable, creating a clear and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11.0 Binding Arbitration Provision: This paragraph is inserted for the purposes of electing binding arbitration which will prevail over the provisions in Paragraphs 9 and 10 with respect to resolution of any dispute. WWWIn order to have this Paragraph 11 in effect, upon execution of this Agreement by both parties in the signature block on Page 5, both parties must sign at the bottom of said Paragraph 11 for this Paragraph 11 to be binding. If both parties do not sign this Paragraph 11 then the provisions of Paragraphs 9 and 10 shall control. If both parties sign below in Paragraph 11 then the provisions of this paragraph with respect to conducting any dispute shall control over the litigation provisions provided in Paragraphs 9 and 10. However, the laws of California shall apply and the arbitration shall take place in Los Angeles, California as set forth below. Upon signing at the bottom of Paragraph 11 in addition to signing on Page 5, the parties agree to submit to binding arbitration in the state of California which shall be a binding arbitration proceeded to be conducted under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association of Los Angeles, California. The subject to binding arbitration will include, without limitation, disputes regarding any breach of contract by either party or any other remedies available to a non-breaching party for failure to perform a condition of this Agreement. The parties agree that such binding arbitration shall be a final decision of the American Arbitration Association. The parties agree that arbitration will be the sole means of resolving such disputes and both parties waive any rights to resolve disputes by a court proceeding or other means and any judgment received in such binding arbitration shall be enforced in the appropriate State or Federal court. In the event of a breach of this Agreement, it is specifically agreed that the non-breaching party may bring a cause of action for a preliminary and permanent injunction in the appropriate state or federal court and thereafter submit the balance of the dispute to binding arbitration if elected under this section. Each party shall pay for one-half the cost of the arbitration. The prevailing party shall be reimbursed for said costs from the non- prevailing party. We specifically elect the arbitration provision in this Agreement: Party 1 : Spa de Soleil Party 2: Business
perturbation.changed_text: 11.0 Binding Arbitration Provision: This paragraph is inserted for the purposes of electing binding arbitration which will not prevail over the provisions in Paragraphs 9 and 10 with respect to resolution of any dispute. In order to have this Paragraph 11 in effect, upon execution of this Agreement by both parties in the signature block on Page 5, both parties do not need to sign at the bottom of said Paragraph 11 for this Paragraph 11 to be binding. If both parties do sign this Paragraph 11 then the provisions of Paragraphs 9 and 10 shall control. Upon signing at the bottom of Paragraph 11 in addition to signing on Page 5, the parties do not agree to submit to binding arbitration in the state of California which shall be a binding arbitration proceeded to be conducted under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association of Los Angeles, California. The parties agree that arbitration will not be the sole means of resolving such disputes and both parties do not waive any rights to resolve disputes by a court proceeding or other means and any judgment received in such binding arbitration shall not be enforced in the appropriate State or Federal court. In the event of a breach of this Agreement, it is specifically agreed that the non-breaching party may not bring a cause of action for a preliminary and permanent injunction in the appropriate state or federal court and thereafter submit the balance of the dispute to binding arbitration if elected under this section. Each party shall pay for one-half the cost of the arbitration. The prevailing party shall not be reimbursed for said costs from the non- prevailing party. We specifically elect the arbitration provision in this Agreement: Party 1 : Spa de Soleil Party 2: Business
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction within Paragraph 11 itself. Initially, the paragraph outlines the conditions under which binding arbitration would be elected and would prevail over Paragraphs 9 and 10. The altered text, states that the binding arbitration will not prevail over Paragraphs 9 and 10, directly contradicting the initial intention. This also contradicts the provision that signing the paragraph means agreeing to binding arbitration. This creates significant confusion about the dispute resolution mechanism. This in-text contradiction also affects Paragraphs 9 and 10 because the original intention of Paragraph 11 was to supplant those paragraphs under certain conditions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.0 Binding Arbitration Provision: This paragraph is inserted for the purposes of electing binding arbitration which will not prevail over the provisions in Paragraphs 9 and 10 with respect to resolution of any dispute. In order to have this Paragraph 11 in effect, upon execution of this Agreement by both parties in the signature block on Page 5, both parties do not need to sign at the bottom of said Paragraph 11 for this Paragraph 11 to be binding. If both parties do sign this Paragraph 11 then the provisions of Paragraphs 9 and 10 shall control. Upon signing at the bottom of Paragraph 11 in addition to signing on Page 5, the parties do not agree to submit to binding arbitration in the state of California which shall be a binding arbitration proceeded to be conducted under the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association of Los Angeles, California. The parties agree that arbitration will not be the sole means of resolving such disputes and both parties do not waive any rights to resolve disputes by a court proceeding or other means and any judgment received in such binding arbitration shall not be enforced in the appropriate State or Federal court. In the event of a breach of this Agreement, it is specifically agreed that the non-breaching party may not bring a cause of action for a preliminary and permanent injunction in the appropriate state or federal court and thereafter submit the balance of the dispute to binding arbitration if elected under this section. Each party shall pay for one-half the cost of the arbitration. The prevailing party shall not be reimbursed for said costs from the non- prevailing party. We specifically elect the arbitration provision in this Agreement: Party 1 : Spa de Soleil Party 2: Business
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within Paragraph 11. It initially states binding arbitration will not prevail over paragraphs 9 and 10, but the original text stated that it would. It also reverses the implications of signing the paragraph, creating direct conflict and inconsistency regarding the dispute resolution process, therefore contradicting the intended meaning and creating a structural flaw based on the perturbation definition. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text is on the same location, however the explanation states how it is contradictory, therefore 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: 13. This Agreement contains the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and supersedes any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement must be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement may not contain the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and does not supersede any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement does not need to be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement is the 'entire understanding' and supersedes prior agreements, and that modifications must be in writing. The changed text reverses these, stating it 'may not contain the entire understanding', 'does not supersede' prior agreements, and modifications 'do not need to be in writing'. This introduces a contradiction about what constitutes the agreement and how it can be changed. This contradiction makes the entire document difficult to rely on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. This Agreement may not contain the entire understanding between the Parties concerning the disclosure of Confidential Information and does not supersede any prior agreements, understandings, or representations with respect thereto. Any addition or modification to this Agreement does not need to be made in writing and signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. It states that the agreement 'may not contain the entire understanding', 'does not supersede' prior agreements, and modifications 'do not need to be in writing'. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text) reinforces this point. The original text, however, says the opposite. Since the changed text opposes the original one and causes the agreement to contradict itself (as it repeats the text of the changed one) there is an in-text contradiction

perturbation.original_text: 16. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterpart, each of which shall be deemed an original but all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterpart, each of which shall be deemed a copy and all of which together shall constitute separate instruments.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines that counterparts are originals and form a single agreement. The changed text defines counterparts as copies and each form a separate agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, making it unclear what a signed counterpart represents and if the document has multiple agreements. This change contradicts the intention that all counterparts together form one and the same agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. This Agreement may be executed in two or more counterpart, each of which shall be deemed a copy and all of which together shall constitute separate instruments.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that each counterpart constitutes 'separate instruments,' while the original text and the explanation indicate that all counterparts together form 'one and the same instrument'. This creates a direct conflict, as the changed text implies multiple agreements exist, contradicting the intention that there is a single agreement. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text, which means that the contradiction occurs in the same location as the change. This enforces a clear contradiction.
